item.title: Computer-Using World Model
item.description: Agents operating in complex software environments benefit from reasoning about the consequences of their actions, as even a single incorrect user interface (UI) operation can derail long, artifact-preserving workflows. This challenge is...

item.title: References Improve LLM Alignment in Non-Verifiable Domains
item.description: While Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has shown strong effectiveness in reasoning tasks, it cannot be directly applied to non-verifiable domains lacking ground-truth verifiers, such as LLM alignment. In this work, w...

item.title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework in Practice: A Risk Analysis Technical Report v1.5
item.description: To understand and identify the unprecedented risks posed by rapidly advancing artificial intelligence (AI) models, Frontier AI Risk Management Framework in Practice presents a comprehensive assessment of their frontier risks. As Large La...

item.title: World Models for Policy Refinement in StarCraft II
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently shown strong reasoning and generalization capabilities, motivating their use as decision-making policies in complex environments. StarCraft II (SC2), with its massive state-action space and part...

item.title: NESSiE: The Necessary Safety Benchmark -- Identifying Errors that should not Exist
item.description: We introduce NESSiE, the NEceSsary SafEty benchmark for large language models (LLMs). With minimal test cases of information and access security, NESSiE reveals safety-relevant failures that should not exist, given the low complexity of...

item.title: Hardware Co-Design Scaling Laws via Roofline Modelling for On-Device LLMs
item.description: Vision-Language-Action Models (VLAs) have emerged as a key paradigm of Physical AI and are increasingly deployed in autonomous vehicles, robots, and smart spaces. In these resource-constrained on-device settings, selecting an appropriate...

item.title: DDiT: Dynamic Patch Scheduling for Efficient Diffusion Transformers
item.description: Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) have achieved state-of-the-art performance in image and video generation, but their success comes at the cost of heavy computation. This inefficiency is largely due to the fixed tokenization process, which u...

item.title: Discovering Multiagent Learning Algorithms with Large Language Models
item.description: Much of the advancement of Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) in imperfect-information games has historically depended on manual iterative refinement of baselines. While foundational families like Counterfactual Regret Minimizatio...

item.title: ArXiv-to-Model: A Practical Study of Scientific LM Training
item.description: While frontier large language models demonstrate strong reasoning and mathematical capabilities, the practical process of training domain-specialized scientific language models from raw sources remains under-documented. In this work, we...

item.title: NeST: Neuron Selective Tuning for LLM Safety
item.description: Safety alignment is essential for the responsible deployment of large language models (LLMs). Yet, existing approaches often rely on heavyweight fine-tuning that is costly to update, audit, and maintain across model families. Full fine-t...

item.title: SpargeAttention2: Trainable Sparse Attention via Hybrid Top-k+Top-p Masking and Distillation Fine-Tuning
item.description: Many training-free sparse attention methods are effective for accelerating diffusion models. Recently, several works suggest that making sparse attention trainable can further increase sparsity while preserving generation quality. We stu...

item.title: Calibrate-Then-Act: Cost-Aware Exploration in LLM Agents
item.description: LLMs are increasingly being used for complex problems which are not necessarily resolved in a single response, but require interacting with an environment to acquire information. In these scenarios, LLMs must reason about inherent cost-u...

item.title: "What Are You Doing?": Effects of Intermediate Feedback from Agentic LLM In-Car Assistants During Multi-Step Processing
item.description: Agentic AI assistants that autonomously perform multi-step tasks raise open questions for user experience: how should such systems communicate progress and reasoning during extended operations, especially in attention-critical contexts s...

item.title: TactAlign: Human-to-Robot Policy Transfer via Tactile Alignment
item.description: Human demonstrations collected by wearable devices (e.g., tactile gloves) provide fast and dexterous supervision for policy learning, and are guided by rich, natural tactile feedback. However, a key challenge is how to transfer human-col...

item.title: Modeling Distinct Human Interaction in Web Agents
item.description: Despite rapid progress in autonomous web agents, human involvement remains essential for shaping preferences and correcting agent behavior as tasks unfold. However, current agentic systems lack a principled understanding of when and why...

item.title: CrispEdit: Low-Curvature Projections for Scalable Non-Destructive LLM Editing
item.description: A central challenge in large language model (LLM) editing is capability preservation: methods that successfully change targeted behavior can quietly game the editing proxy and corrupt general capabilities, producing degenerate behaviors...

item.title: Mobile-Agent-v3.5: Multi-platform Fundamental GUI Agents
item.description: The paper introduces GUI-Owl-1.5, the latest native GUI agent model that features instruct/thinking variants in multiple sizes (2B/4B/8B/32B/235B) and supports a range of platforms (desktop, mobile, browser, and more) to enable cloud-edg...

item.title: FRAPPE: Infusing World Modeling into Generalist Policies via Multiple Future Representation Alignment
item.description: Enabling VLA models to predict environmental dynamics, known as world modeling, has been recognized as essential for improving robotic reasoning and generalization. However, current approaches face two main issues: 1. The training object...

item.title: Unified Latents (UL): How to train your latents
item.description: We present Unified Latents (UL), a framework for learning latent representations that are jointly regularized by a diffusion prior and decoded by a diffusion model. By linking the encoder's output noise to the prior's minimum noise level...

item.title: Arcee Trinity Large Technical Report
item.description: We present the technical report for Arcee Trinity Large, a sparse Mixture-of-Experts model with 400B total parameters and 13B activated per token. Additionally, we report on Trinity Nano and Trinity Mini, with Trinity Nano having 6B tota...

item.title: 2Mamba2Furious: Linear in Complexity, Competitive in Accuracy
item.description: Linear attention transformers have become a strong alternative to softmax attention due to their efficiency. However, linear attention tends to be less expressive and results in reduced accuracy compared to softmax attention. To bridge t...

item.title: On the Mechanism and Dynamics of Modular Addition: Fourier Features, Lottery Ticket, and Grokking
item.description: We present a comprehensive analysis of how two-layer neural networks learn features to solve the modular addition task. Our work provides a full mechanistic interpretation of the learned model and a theoretical explanation of its trainin...

item.title: StereoAdapter-2: Globally Structure-Consistent Underwater Stereo Depth Estimation
item.description: Stereo depth estimation is fundamental to underwater robotic perception, yet suffers from severe domain shifts caused by wavelength-dependent light attenuation, scattering, and refraction. Recent approaches leverage monocular foundation...
